title: Cartlane
summary: A production-built commerce checkout proving Octane native form actions, pending state, idempotent server functions, SSR, and hydration.

journeys.title: Adopt a server-rendered product input, preserve its value, and add to the basket by keyboard
Retry the catalog, recover from an empty filter, and use the mobile basket offline
Reject a tampered cart, validate native checkout, and place an order through a server function
Queue rapid checkout submissions and resolve both to one charged order
Preserve checkout details through a payment decline and an offline retry

faultScenarios.description: An opt-in delay leaves the real server product form interactive before hydration so adoption and typed-value preservation are observable.
The scenario query fails one deterministic collection load and succeeds on explicit retry without changing the basket.
The local server function rejects the documented 4000 fixture card while preserving every uncontrolled native form field for retry.
A negative quantity is injected into the submitted cart; the server-owned catalog rejects it without an order or charge before a valid retry succeeds.
A disconnected browser retains the basket and checkout fields, reports the blocked action, and safely retries after reconnecting.
Two rapid native submissions cross the RPC boundary but share one idempotency key and resolve to one order and one charge.
